title: Jenn

{{Quote|What's the matter, Till? Don't tell me a hotshot pilot like you is afraid of the Dreighton Triangle.|Jenn|audio=Jenn-RAII.flac|<ref name="RAII" />}}
'''Jenn''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] who served as a [[Commander/Legends|commander]] in the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. She was [[Death/Legends|killed]] during a routine patrol of the [[Dreighton Triangle]] when one of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] new [[TIE/ph "Phantom" Multi-Role Stealth Starfighter/Legends|TIE Phantom]] starfighters used its [[Cloaking device/Legends|cloaking device]] to take her by surprise, damaging her [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing]] and causing her to collide with [[Ensign/Legends|Ensign]] [[Till]]'s fighter.

==Biography==
The [[Human/Legends|Human]]<ref name="RAII" /> [[Gender/Legends|female]]<ref name="LA" /> Jenn was born around [[25 BBY/Legends|25 BBY]], during the final years of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]].<ref name="age" /> At some point before [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],<ref name="NEC" /> Jenn joined the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] as a [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]]<ref name="RAII" /> and rose to the rank of [[Commander/Legends|commander]].<ref name="LA" />

Following the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]] in 3 ABY,<ref name="NEC" /> the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] entered the final stages of development for the [[TIE/ph "Phantom" Multi-Role Stealth Starfighter/Legends|TIE Phantom]], a new starfighter which was fitted with a [[Cloaking device/Legends|cloaking device]] that rendered it invisible to other [[Starship/Legends|vessels]]. As the project neared completion, the Empire began a series of tests in the region of [[Realspace/Legends|space]] known as the [[Dreighton Triangle]], using reports of unexplained events in the region ever since the [[Battle of Dreighton]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] as a cover for their operations. Around this time, Jenn was tasked to lead a [[Flight group/Legends|flight group]] of three [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|T-65 X-wing starfighters]], including one flown by [[Ensign/Legends|Ensign]] [[Till]], on a patrol mission around the [[Dreighton Nebula/Legends|Dreighton Nebula]]. At first the group's [[Sensor/Legends|sensors]] failed to detect any signs of activity but Jenn ordered one more pass before returning to base. Moments later, a TIE Phantom decloaked behind<ref name="RAII" /> her<ref name="LA" /> X-wing and opened fire, tearing the starboard [[S-foils/Legends|S-foils]] from the fuselage and disabling her [[Stabilizer/Legends|stabilizer]]. Unable to control her fighter, Jenn drifted to port and collided with Till's X-wing, destroying both fighters and [[Death/Legends|killing]] both pilots.<ref name="RAII" />

==Personality and traits==
Jenn was a professional [[Officer/Legends|officer]] who was brave, intelligent, and who possessed a good sense of humor. She was good friends with Till and frequently teased the other pilot.<ref name="LA" /> On one such occasion, Jenn mocked Till's apparent fear of the [[ghost ship]] stories regarding the Dreighton Triangle.<ref name="RAII" />

==Behind the scenes==
Jenn was created for the [[1995]] [[LucasArts]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Rebel Assault II: The Hidden Empire]]'', in which she appears during the opening cutscene. The character was portrayed in the game by actress [[Zachary Barton]].<ref name="RAII" />

[[File:Y-wing_Helmet_ROTJ.jpg|thumb|right|220px|Y-wing pilot helmet from ''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'' reused for Jenn]]
''Rebel Assault II'' designer [[Vincent Lee]]'s storyboards and scripts document, dated [[November 16]], [[1994]], included a brief description of Jenn.<ref name="RL">''[[Rogue Leaders: The Story of LucasArts]]''</ref> Though not included in the game or its manual,<ref name="RAII" /> the biography was adapted for use on the LucasArts website<ref name="LA" /> and the document was later printed in [[Rob Smith]]'s ''[[Rogue Leaders: The Story of LucasArts]]''.<ref name="RL" /> The version that appeared online places Jenn in her late twenties during the game<ref name="LA" /> whereas Lee's original version described her as "late twenties to early thirties." Lee also made notes on the people he thought symbolized each character in the game, choosing actress [[Wikipedia:Holly Hunter|Holly Hunter]] to represent Jenn. An early storyboard of the scene in which Jenn appears reveals that her flight group was originally called "[[Gamma]] patrol,"<ref name="RL" /> but the group was unnamed in the final game.<ref name="RAII" />

Props and costumes created for the [[original trilogy]] of ''[[Star Wars]]'' movies were used during the filming of ''Rebel Assault II''.<ref name="LAV">{{LucasArts|url=products/rebel2/reb_vid.htm|text=''Rebel Assault II'' - Live Action Video}}</ref> The [[Flight helmet/Legends|flight helmet]] worn by Jenn was one originally created for a [[BTL Y-wing starfighter/Legends|Y-wing]] pilot in [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']].<ref name="SWC">''[[Star Wars Costumes: The Original Trilogy]]''</ref>
